headline: Prepared parent sandbox branches into coupon debugging hypotheses.

agentPattern.title: Better fork point: after useful disk or memory state exists
agentPattern.originalComment: Imagine an agent that has already identified the files responsible for a failing test. Rather than assigning three agents to independently rediscover the same information, a single agent performs the exploration once before forking into specialized workers.
agentPattern.feedbackIncorporated: The demo makes that stronger by having the parent write durable state first: failing logs, debug brief, state manifest, benchmark output, and a candidate shared-contract fix. It also includes a memory probe that attempts to fork after warming live process memory, while logging the observed result instead of overclaiming support.
candidateFixState: src/shared/couponContract.js changed to validate against seeded coupon data
debug-brief.md updated with the winning contract hypothesis
baseline failure reproduced once for comparison
state-manifest.json records the prepared parent state
fix-candidate.md explains why this branch should be reviewed
optional memory-agent process warmed before the memory probe fork

nodes.name: coupon-parent-prepared
nodes.summary: Live Daytona parent contains prepared coupon state before fork.

nodes.name: frontend-hypothesis
nodes.summary: Live child fork wrote frontend-specific fork-note.txt.
nodes.testStatus: UI improves; API contract still fails

nodes.name: backend-hypothesis
nodes.summary: Live child fork wrote backend-specific fork-note.txt.

nodes.name: contract-hypothesis
nodes.summary: Live child fork wrote contract-specific fork-note.txt.

nodes.name: contract-with-regression-test
nodes.summary: Shared fix plus explicit invalid coupon regression coverage.

forkTreeLevels.title: Prepared parent
forkTreeLevels.why: The parent is useful before branching: repo, dependencies, build, seeded data, failing logs, debug brief, marker, and benchmark output already exist.

nodes.name: coupon-parent-prepared
nodes.whyItMatters: Live Daytona parent now contains prepared coupon state before fork.

forkTreeLevels.title: Hypothesis forks
forkTreeLevels.why: Three independent futures branch from the same prepared disk state and test different explanations.

nodes.name: frontend-hypothesis
nodes.whyItMatters: Live child fork wrote frontend-specific fork-note.txt.

nodes.name: backend-hypothesis
nodes.whyItMatters: Live child fork wrote backend-specific fork-note.txt.

nodes.name: contract-hypothesis
nodes.whyItMatters: Live contract branch wrote fix-candidate.md and became the next fork point.

forkTreeLevels.title: Fork from the changed contract branch
forkTreeLevels.why: The winning branch is not just copied from the original baseline; it becomes a new parent after writing the candidate fix.

nodes.name: contract-candidate-fix
nodes.whyItMatters: Live branch now has changed filesystem state that refinements and validators inherit.

nodes.name: contract-minimal
nodes.whyItMatters: Live refinement inherits the candidate contract fix and isolates the smallest patch.

nodes.name: contract-with-regression-test
nodes.whyItMatters: Live refinement inherits the candidate contract fix and adds regression coverage separately.

forkTreeLevels.title: Validator fan-out from candidate state
forkTreeLevels.why: Specialized workers start from the actual changed branch, so they review the fix instead of rediscovering the bug.

nodes.name: api-design-review
nodes.whyItMatters: Live API reviewer green-lit invalid coupon behavior.

nodes.name: implementation-review
nodes.whyItMatters: Live implementation reviewer green-lit the minimal shared contract fix.

nodes.name: regression-test-builder
nodes.whyItMatters: Live test builder green-lit explicit invalid coupon coverage.

forkTreeLevels.title: Runtime-state observation
forkTreeLevels.why: A memory probe branches after a process is warmed, then records what the runner actually preserved.

nodes.name: memory-state-check
nodes.whyItMatters: Live child observed the warmed memory-agent state for this run.

cloneFanOut.title: Three concurrent clones
cloneFanOut.summary: All workers start from the original baseline and must recreate or receive the intermediate fix context.
cloneFanOut.tree:
baseline
├── clone A: API review rediscovers context
├── clone B: implementation review rediscovers context
└── clone C: tests rebuild candidate assumptions
limits: No clone naturally inherits the changed contract branch unless you rebuild or ship that state.
The candidate fix is not a new branch point; it is duplicated manually.
A failed validator has a less precise parent to return to.

forkedStateTree.title: Multi-level fork tree
forkedStateTree.summary: The contract branch writes the candidate fix first, then validators fork from that exact changed state.
forkedStateTree.tree:
prepared baseline
└── contract-hypothesis
    └── contract-candidate-fix
        ├── api-design-review
        ├── implementation-review
        ├── regression-test-builder
benefits: Intermediate work becomes the next fork point.
Validators inherit files, logs, and candidate fix artifacts directly.

validatorWorkers.name: api-design-review
validatorWorkers.strategy: Check whether the API contract now reports Invalid coupon code before checkout.
validatorWorkers.inherits: candidate shared-contract fix + failing baseline log + debug brief

validatorWorkers.name: implementation-review
validatorWorkers.strategy: Inspect edge cases in normalization, seeded coupon lookup, and checkout totals.
validatorWorkers.inherits: candidate shared-contract fix + seeded coupon data + build artifacts

validatorWorkers.name: regression-test-builder
validatorWorkers.strategy: Add tests that prove SUMMER-2026 fails during validation, not checkout.
validatorWorkers.inherits: candidate shared-contract fix + test suite + failing baseline output

memoryProbe.name: memory-state-check
memoryProbe.whatChangesBeforeFork: A Node process increments in-memory state without writing that counter to disk.
memoryProbe.expectedObservation: If live process memory is preserved by this fork operation, the child can query the warmed memory-agent process.

forkEvents.result: wrote repo packet, seeded data, build marker, debug brief, failing logs, state manifest, and benchmark JSON

forkEvents.action: write contract fork note and fix-candidate.md

forkEvents.result: minimal fix refinement created from contract candidate
regression-test refinement created from contract candidate

steps: state packet, logs, marker, manifest, and benchmark exist
two live refinements created from contract branch
contract branch wrote fix-candidate.md before more forks

terminals.title: daytona inspect-state
terminals.output:
repo present: yes
dependencies installed: yes
build artifacts present: yes
seeded coupon data present: yes

failing baseline log present: yes

env configured: yes
app can start: yes

terminals.title: advanced Daytona probe
terminals.command: optional: open the advanced Daytona probe and enter a dtn_ key
terminals.output: This is separate from the recommended recording path. npm run demo:agent uses the OpenAI key from .env; the advanced probe expects a Daytona dtn_ key.
